flashcards.back: Java Server Pages is a server-side technology that embeds Java code in HTML pages to create dynamic web content. It separates presentation from business logic.

flashcards.front: List the phases of JSP lifecycle
flashcards.back: 1. Translation (JSP to servlet) 2. Compilation 3. Initialization 4. Execution 5. Destruction
<%! declaration %> - used to declare variables/methods at servlet class level
<% code %> inserts Java code into _jspService() method. Used for business logic implementation.
request, response, session, out, application, pageContext, page, exception, config (any three)

flashcards.front: Difference between include directive and include action
flashcards.back: Directive: <%@ include file="..." %> (compile-time inclusion). Action: <jsp:include> (runtime inclusion)
JavaServer Pages Standard Tag Library provides ready-made tags for iteration, conditionals, formatting, and SQL operations.

flashcards.front: EL expression to access bean property
flashcards.back: ${bean.property} - Automatically calls getProperty() method of the JavaBean
Instantiates or locates a JavaBean object with specified scope (page, request, session, application)
JspWriter object used to write content to client's response stream. Similar to PrintWriter in servlets.

flashcards.front: How to handle errors in JSP?
flashcards.back: Use <%@ page errorPage="error.jsp" %> in main JSP and <%@ page isErrorPage="true" %> in error page
<%-- comment --%> - Comments not visible in client-side HTML
Model 1: JSP handles both view & control. Model 2 (MVC): Servlets (controller), JSP (view), JavaBeans (model)
1. Create tag handler class 2. Define TLD file 3. Declare taglib in JSP using <%@ taglib %>
HttpSession object that maintains user-specific data across multiple requests
Transfers request processing to another resource (JSP/servlet) on the server

flashcards.front: How to set cookies in JSP?
flashcards.back: response.addCookie(new Cookie("name","value")) in scriptlet or using JSTL <c:set>
Provides access to all namespaces (page, request, session, application) and page attributes
${param.username} retrieves value of 'username' parameter from request
ServletContext object representing entire web application. Shared across all users/sessions

flashcards.front: How to import classes in JSP?
flashcards.back: <%@ page import="java.util.*, com.example.MyClass" %> in page directive
Iterates over collections/arrays. Syntax: <c:forEach items="${list}" var="item">

flashcards.front: Main advantage of JSP over servlets
flashcards.back: Easier HTML integration - Java code can be embedded directly in HTML using JSP tags
Defines page attributes: <%@ page contentType="text/html", errorPage="...", import="..." %>
JSP container (like Tomcat) automatically translates JSP to servlet during first request
